title: Grammy Best New Artist: All the winners including Olivia Dean, Chappell Roan
text: Olivia Dean became the 34th solo female artist and ninth consecutive woman to claim Best New Artist. She performed Man I Need during the 2026 ceremony. The award debuted at the second Grammy ceremony in 1959, with Bobby Darin as the first recipient. Notable aspects include: Solo female artists have dominated the category, winning 34 times as of 2026. Groups and collaborative acts have won 18 times. Solo male performers have won only 12 times. The category has produced both major stars and cautionary tales. The Beatles, Bette Midler, Cyndi Lauper, Mariah Carey, and Adele achieved sustained success, while acts like Starland Vocal Band, A Taste of Honey, and Christopher Cross struggled to replicate initial achievements. This remains the only Grammy category awarded for an artist's cumulative work and doesn't cite a specific album, composition, or performance. The award was vacated in 1989—the only time in Grammy history—following the Milli Vanilli lip-sync scandal.
